_description: Configure Rust PyO3 modules and calculator definitions for high-performance native computation

rust_manager._enabled_note: Set to true to enable automatic Rust module loading and calculator initialization
rust_manager._auto_load_note: If true, Rust modules are loaded when application.properties is loaded
rust_manager._config_check_note: Check for config file changes every 600 seconds (10 minutes)

modules.name: dishtayantra_rust
modules._path_note: Path to the .so/.pyd file. If null, expects module in Python path
modules.description: Core DishtaYantra Rust calculators with memory safety guarantees

build._build_note: Build settings for automatic compilation (requires Rust and maturin)

lmdb._lmdb_note: LMDB zero-copy transport for large payloads (>10KB)

calculators.name: RustPassthroughCalculator
calculators.description: Passes input data through unchanged - useful for testing and benchmarking

calculators.name: RustMathCalculator
calculators.description: High-performance mathematical operations with SIMD optimizations

calculators.name: RustStringCalculator
calculators.description: Memory-safe string operations with zero-copy support

calculators.name: RustDataValidationCalculator
calculators.description: Memory-safe data validation with regex support

calculators.name: RustTradePricingCalculator
calculators.description: Thread-safe trade pricing with concurrent access

calculators.name: RustRiskMetricsCalculator
calculators.description: Financial risk metrics with parallel Monte Carlo

calculators.name: RustTimeSeriesCalculator
calculators.description: Time series analysis with moving averages and trends

performance._simd_note: Enable SIMD optimizations where available (AVX, SSE, NEON)
performance._rayon_note: Number of Rayon threads for parallel operations (0 = auto)
performance._gil_note: Release Python GIL during computations for true parallelism

_documentation.overview: This configuration defines Rust PyO3 modules and calculators available to DishtaYantra DAGs
_documentation.module_structure: Each module is a compiled .so/.pyd file containing one or more calculator classes
_documentation.calculator_interface: Rust calculators must implement: new(name, config), calculate(data), details()
_documentation.memory_safety: Rust guarantees memory safety and thread safety at compile time
